ocr.text: Table 1. The major triterpenoids in A. cinnamomea. M.W., Molecular weight (g/mol).
Figure 2. Antiproliferative effects of sorafenib and EAC in HCC cell lines. (A) Huh-7 cells and (B) HepG2 cells
were exposed to sorafenib and EAC at the indicated concentrations in 10% FBS-Supplemented DMEM for 24
and 48 h, and cell viability was assessed by MTT assays. Columns, mean; bars, SD (n = 6). *P < 0.05; **P < 0.01;
***P < 0.001. (C) The IC50 values of sorafenib and EAC in two HCC cell lines after treatment for 48 h and cell
viability was measured by MTT assay.
EAC sensitizes HCC cells to the cytotoxicity of sorafenib. To examine the ability of EAC to boost
sorafenib’s anticancer activity, HCC cells were treated with different concentrations of sorafenib with or without
EAC and the cell viability was assessed by MTT assay. The results indicated that EAC dose-dependently signifi-
cantly sensitized Huh-7 and HepG2 cells to sorafenib-induced cell death (Fig. 3A, B). The combination index (CI)
